creator: nemysis@gmx.ch
text:
Allegro is a cross-platform library intended for use in computer games and
other types of multimedia programming.

A wide range of extension packages and add-on modules are also available, which
can be found in the "Library Extensions" section of the Allegro website.

WWW: http://www.talula.demon.co.uk/allegro/

Allegro 5.0.7 is different from 4.4.2.

Generated and tested manually, tested with port test, sent with FreeBSD Port Tools 0.99_6 (mode: new)

How-To-Repeat: 
portlint -A
WARN: Makefile: for new port, make $FreeBSD$ tag in comment section empty, to make SVN happy.
WARN: Makefile: LIB_DEPENDS don't specify the ABI version number .2 in asound.2 unless it is really necessary.
WARN: Makefile: LIB_DEPENDS don't specify the ABI version number .0 in jack.0 unless it is really necessary.
WARN: Makefile: LIB_DEPENDS don't specify the ABI version number .1 in physfs.1 unless it is really necessary.
0 fatal errors and 4 warnings found.


port test: clean


Build log

https://redports.org/buildarchive/20121022105605-18971/

Can't be builded on RedPorts because audio/jack, graphics/svgalib, print/ghostscript9 not build


There is too ports/170448 [NEW PORT] devel/allegro5: Allegro 5 is a game programming libraries, primarily for C and C++
which i have looked, but port test and install not works with it.
